mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 3.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE increases by >0.3pp in 1 month (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in inflation expectations signals economic overheating, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to heightened recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO falls >1.5% and CPIAUCSL rises >0.5% in 1 month (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising consumer spending signals robust demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings improve.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly positive when HOUST and RSAFS both rise >2% in 1 month (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market liquidity contracts.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth indicates a resilient labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate profit expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS rises >100k in 1 month (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDP growth combined with rising consumer confidence signals an economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 falls >0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises >0.3% in 1 month (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals stronger supply-side capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO rises >1.5% in 1 month (t-test p > 0.05).
